channel.title: News - English [KBS WORLD Radio] | International
channel.description: We provide you with the latest news from home and abroad, in addition to in-depth analysis on major issues, with a close eye on Northeast Asia and the Korean Peninsula in particular.

item.title: S. Korea Points to Iran in Hormuz Ship Attack, Plans to Summon Ambassador
item.description:
[International] :
The government has concluded that Iran was likely behind the May 4 attack on the South Korean-operated cargo ship the HMM Namu, which was struck while stranded in the Strait of Hormuz amid the U.S.-Iran conflict. First Vice Foreign Minister Park Yoon-joo announced on Wednesday that the findings of the ...

[more...]

image.title: S. Korea Points to Iran in Hormuz Ship Attack, Plans to Summon Ambassador

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Quad Nations Reaffirm Commitment to N. Korea's Complete Denuclearization
item.description:
[International] :
Top diplomats from the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue have reaffirmed commitment to the complete denuclearization of North Korea and urged Pyongyang to abide by all its obligations under relevant United Nations Security Council resolutions. In a joint statement following a foreign ministers’ meeting in ...

[more...]

image.title: Quad Nations Reaffirm Commitment to N. Korea's Complete Denuclearization

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: S. Korea and China Hold 3rd Export Control Dialogue
item.description:
[International] :
South Korea and China on Monday held their third round of dialogue on export control in the Chinese city of Xian.  The Ministry of Trade, Industry and Resources announced on Tuesday that the two sides agreed to boost dialogue and cooperation in export control via extensive discussions on rare earths, ...

[more...]

image.title: S. Korea and China Hold 3rd Export Control Dialogue

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: KDCA Includes Ethiopia, Rwanda as Priority Quarantine Management Regions for Ebola
item.description:
[International] :
The Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency(KDCA) has included Ethiopia and Rwanda as regions subject to its priority quarantine management for the Ebola virus. Following the latest inclusion on Tuesday, five countries are on the list of priority quarantine management — the Democratic Republic of ...

[more...]

image.title: KDCA Includes Ethiopia, Rwanda as Priority Quarantine Management Regions for Ebola

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: S. Korean Submarine Dosan Ahn Chang-ho Welcomed in Canada after Crossing Pacific for First Time
item.description:
[International] :
A welcoming ceremony has taken place in Canada for a South Korean-built naval submarine that crossed the Pacific Ocean for the first time in history to participate in combined naval exercises. The three-thousand-ton-class ROKS Dosan Ahn Chang-ho had traveled approximately 14-thousand kilometers from Jinhae ...

[more...]

image.title: S. Korean Submarine Dosan Ahn Chang-ho Welcomed in Canada after Crossing Pacific for First Time

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: US Strikes Southern Iran amid Ceasefire Extension Negotiations
item.description:
[International] :
U.S. forces have struck targets in southern Iran in an action they said was defensive in nature. U.S. Central Command(CENTCOM) issued a statement Monday saying the strikes against boats attempting ​to lay mines and ⁠missile-launch sites were designed to protect ‌American troops from “threats posed ...

[more...]

image.title: US Strikes Southern Iran amid Ceasefire Extension Negotiations

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Iran Confirms Progress in Talks with US; Agreement Not Imminent
item.description:
[International] :
Iran says it has made progress in the negotiations for a memorandum of understanding to end the war with the U.S., but that an agreement is “not imminent.” Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei said in a briefing on Monday that the two sides made progress “on a large portion of the ...

[more...]

image.title: Iran Confirms Progress in Talks with US; Agreement Not Imminent

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Iran Confirms Progress in US Talks but Says No Deal Imminent, Disputes Nuclear Claims
item.description:
[International] :
Anchor: A preliminary agreement between the United States and Iran has reportedly been reached in relation to ending the war. While the U.S. claims the agreement would require Iran to give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, Iran says no deal is imminent and the two sides are not talking about ...

[more...]

image.title: Iran Confirms Progress in US Talks but Says No Deal Imminent, Disputes Nuclear Claims

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: WP: US, Iran Framework Deal Includes Strait of Hormuz Reopening within 30 Days
item.description:
[International] :
The United States and Iran have developed a memorandum of understanding framework that would extend the current ceasefire by 60 days, with the Strait of Hormuz to be demined and reopened as part of the deal, the Washington Post reported Sunday.  Under the latest proposal, which is awaiting Iranian ...

[more...]

image.title: WP: US, Iran Framework Deal Includes Strait of Hormuz Reopening within 30 Days

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Suspect Killed After Opening Fire on Secret Service Security Checkpoint Outside White House
item.description:
[International] :
A person who opened fire on officers at a Secret Service security checkpoint outside the White House was shot and killed by security personnel, authorities said. The United States Secret Service said in a statement Saturday that the individual approached the checkpoint at 17th Street and Pennsylvania ...

[more...]

image.title: Suspect Killed After Opening Fire on Secret Service Security Checkpoint Outside White House

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Trump: Deal with Iran to be Announced Soon
item.description:
[International] :
President Donald Trump said Friday that a deal between the United States and Iran to end the war has been "largely negotiated" and will be announced soon. In a post on Truth Social, Trump said final details are still being discussed and will be released shortly. He did not provide additional ...

[more...]

image.title: Trump: Deal with Iran to be Announced Soon

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: UN Nuclear Treaty Review Conference Again Fails to Adopt Consensus Document
item.description:
[International] :
The 11th Review Conference of the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty, or NPT, ended Friday at U.N. headquarters in New York without adopting a final consensus document. Member states failed to bridge differences over regional nuclear issues involving North Korea and Iran, as well as nuclear disarmament ...

[more...]

image.title: UN Nuclear Treaty Review Conference Again Fails to Adopt Consensus Document

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Chinese Daily Positively Assesses Lee's Strong Stance against Anti-China Sentiment
item.description:
[International] :
A Chinese daily has suggested that Seoul-Beijing relations could improve after President Lee Jae Myung dismissed recent South Korean media reports about Chinese nationals buying up apartments in Seoul’s affluent Gangnam District as “fake news.” China's state-run English daily Global Times said ...

[more...]

image.title: Chinese Daily Positively Assesses Lee's Strong Stance against Anti-China Sentiment

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: WP: New US Guidelines Require Foreigners to Apply for Green Cards Outside US
item.description:
[International] :
U.S. immigrations authorities have announced new guidelines stipulating that most foreigners seeking green cards would have to return to their home country to apply, with exceptions limited to "extraordinary circumstances." According to The Washington Post on Friday, the guidelines issued by the U.S. ...

[more...]

image.title: WP: New US Guidelines Require Foreigners to Apply for Green Cards Outside US

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Global Oil Prices Slightly Rise amid Concerns over Delay in US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description:
[International] :
Global oil prices slightly rose on Friday amid concerns over a delay in peace talks between the United States and Iran. Brent crude, the international standard, for July delivery ended Friday at 103 U.S. dollars and 54 cents per barrel, up zero-point-94 percent from the previous session's ...

[more...]

image.title: Global Oil Prices Slightly Rise amid Concerns over Delay in US-Iran Peace Talks

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: S. Korea Tightens Travel Alerts for Congo, Uganda over Ebola Outbreak
item.description:
[International] :
The government has issued a travel ban for parts of the Democratic Republic of the Congo amid the spread of the Ebola virus. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs said Friday that a Level 4 travel advisory, the highest level corresponding to a travel ban, will take effect for Ituri Province in the African ...

[more...]

image.title: S. Korea Tightens Travel Alerts for Congo, Uganda over Ebola Outbreak

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: WP: Shortage of US THAAD Interceptors Alarming for S. Korea, Japan
item.description:
[International] :
A leading U.S. daily says the U.S. military has depleted much of its inventory of Terminal High Altitude Area Defense(THAAD) interceptors in defense of Israel, raising security concerns for South Korea and Japan.  The Washington Post on Thursday quoted U.S. officials as saying the U.S. launched more than ...

[more...]

image.title: WP: Shortage of US THAAD Interceptors Alarming for S. Korea, Japan

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: 2 Pro-Palestinian Activists Return Home to S. Korea after Israel Intercepts Gaza Aid Flotilla
item.description:
[International] :
Anchor: Two South Korean nationals have landed safely at Incheon International Airport after an ordeal at the hands of Israeli forces. Both pro-Palestinian activists were captured earlier this week while en route to Gaza to provide humanitarian aid as part of an international flotilla. One says she was ...

[more...]

image.title: 2 Pro-Palestinian Activists Return Home to S. Korea after Israel Intercepts Gaza Aid Flotilla

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Orders No Transfer of Highly Enriched Uranium
item.description:
[International] :
Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has reportedly issued a directive that the country's near-weapons-grade uranium not be transferred abroad, rejecting a key United States demand in peace negotiations. Citing two senior Iranian sources on Thursday, Reuters reported that Iran's top officials believe ...

[more...]

image.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Orders No Transfer of Highly Enriched Uranium

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Vice Foreign Minister Says Seoul, Washington Committed to Speeding up Implementation of Security Cooperation
item.description:
[International] :
Vice Foreign Minister Park Yoon-joo told reporters in Washington on Wednesday that South Korea and the United States are committed to accelerating security cooperation, as noted in a joint fact sheet issued by Seoul and Washington in November. During a news briefing in Washington on Wednesday local time, ...

[more...]

image.title: Vice Foreign Minister Says Seoul, Washington Committed to Speeding up Implementation of Security Cooperation

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: 2 S. Korean Activists Released by Israel to Return Friday
item.description:
[International] :
Two South Korean activists released after being apprehended by Israeli forces aboard aid ships heading to Gaza are set to return home Friday via Thailand. Foreign ministry spokesperson Park Il said Thursday that the two South Korean nationals — Kim Ah-hyun and Kim Dong-hyeon — would arrive Friday ...

[more...]

image.title: 2 S. Korean Activists Released by Israel to Return Friday

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: S. Korea Expects Final OPCON Verification to Be Completed Next Year
item.description:
[International] :
South Korea believes that the final verification stage for the transfer of wartime operational control(OPCON) from the United States can be completed in about one year, potentially paving the way for the transition as early as next year. According to an official at the defense ministry, who declined to be ...

[more...]

image.title: S. Korea Expects Final OPCON Verification to Be Completed Next Year

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: US Ambassador Nominee Stresses Bolstering Seoul-Washington Alliance
item.description:
[International] :
Michelle Steel, the nominee for U.S. ambassador to South Korea, has emphasized bolstering the alliance between the two nations while pledging to ensure that American companies do not face discrimination or unnecessary barriers in the South Korean market. During her U.S. Senate confirmation hearing on ...

[more...]

image.title: US Ambassador Nominee Stresses Bolstering Seoul-Washington Alliance

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Xi, Putin Urge End to Sanctions against N. Korea
item.description:
[International] :
Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin have called for efforts to maintain peace and stability on the Korean Peninsula, and for an end to sanctions against North Korea. According to a joint statement published on Wednesday by the state-run Xinhua News Agency after a summit ...

[more...]

image.title: Xi, Putin Urge End to Sanctions against N. Korea

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Israeli Military Seizes Another Gaza Aid Flotilla Carrying S. Korean Activist
item.description:
[International] :
The government has requested Israel to swiftly release and deport a South Korean activist who was aboard a Gaza aid flotilla that was intercepted by the Israeli military.  A foreign ministry official said Wednesday that Seoul has continuously asked Israel, through various channels, to ensure the swift ...

[more...]

image.title: Israeli Military Seizes Another Gaza Aid Flotilla Carrying S. Korean Activist

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Takaichi: S. Korea-Japan Cooperation on Supply Chains Crucial Given Int’l Situation
item.description:
[International] :
Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi says South Korea-Japan cooperation on supply chains, including supply chains for critical minerals, is important given the recent international situation.  Takaichi made the remark on Tuesday in a joint press statement released after her summit with South Korean ...

[more...]

image.title: Takaichi: S. Korea-Japan Cooperation on Supply Chains Crucial Given Int’l Situation

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Leaders of S. Korea, Japan Reaffirm Importance of Bilateral Cooperation, 3-Way Cooperation with US
item.description:
[International] :
President Lee Jae Myung says he and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reaffirmed the importance of bilateral cooperation and three-way cooperation with the U.S. for the sake of peace and stability in the region amid the fast-changing state of international affairs. Lee delivered the information Tuesday ...

[more...]

image.title: Leaders of S. Korea, Japan Reaffirm Importance of Bilateral Cooperation, 3-Way Cooperation with US

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: S. Korea, Japan Agree to Expand Cooperation in Supply Chains and Energy
item.description:
[International] :
President Lee Jae Myung and Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi have agreed that peace and stability in the Middle East must be swiftly restored.  Lee disclosed their conclusion during a joint press statement released after his summit with Takaichi in his hometown of Andong, North Gyeongsang ...

[more...]

image.title: S. Korea, Japan Agree to Expand Cooperation in Supply Chains and Energy

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Lee: S. Korea-Japan Partnership More Important than Ever amid Turbulent State of Global Affairs
item.description:
[International] :
President Lee Jae Myung has stressed the need for South Korea and Japan to strengthen cooperation, considering the turbulent state of international affairs. Lee expressed that view on Tuesday in his opening remarks at the summit with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi in his hometown of Andong, North ...

[more...]

image.title: Lee: S. Korea-Japan Partnership More Important than Ever amid Turbulent State of Global Affairs

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio

item.title: Iran Suggests HMM Namu Attack May Have Been ‘False Flag’
item.description:
[International] :
Anchor: The Iranian foreign ministry has stated its position on an attack on a South Korean cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this month, saying it too has questions about the incident. While claiming ignorance, Tehran suggested the attack may have been an attempt by some unknown party to escalate ...

[more...]

image.title: Iran Suggests HMM Namu Attack May Have Been ‘False Flag’

item.author: KBS WORLD Radio
